content: The hard bound on an agent’s working memory capacity that limits reasoning is not a bug to be fixed with larger architectures, but a functional constraint that can be leveraged to drive structured episodic memory growth in curiosity-driven planning agents. Just as limited working memory forces humans to offload repeated or high-stakes reasoning patterns to long-term episodic memory for later retrieval, AI agents can use explicit working memory overflow as a curiosity signal to prioritize which new episodes to encode and rehearse for future planning.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
neural architecture memory episodic planning agent
